topic.what: Neural leakage-based key recovery attack on LowMC block cipher used in MPC-in-the-Head signatures (PICNIC/AIMer)
topic.how: Perturbation-based probing methodology models activation-boundary leakage; reduces round-key recovery to independent binary hypothesis tests via majority voting; exploits linear key schedule for master key reconstruction
topic.why_matters: Demonstrates new attack vector against MPCitH post-quantum signatures; reveals that neural/hybrid implementations may leak via activation boundaries not captured by classical Boolean security models

characteristics.what_it_measures: Successful master key recovery from recovered round key
characteristics.evidence: Experimental results confirm successful recovery of 128-, 192-, and 256-bit keys
characteristics.context: With Grain-SSG full rank; 100% success from recovered rk1 to master key

characteristics.what_it_measures: Algorithm complexity for full key recovery
characteristics.evidence: First-round key recovery enables efficient master key reconstruction with linear complexity
characteristics.context: Exploiting linear structure of LowMC key schedule

characteristics.what_it_measures: Round-key bit estimation accuracy via majority voting
characteristics.evidence: Majority voting approximates Neyman-Pearson detector for binary hypothesis testing
characteristics.context: Each bit recovered via 1-neuron perceptron/threshold classifier
